projectDescription: A Rust typestate framework that makes invalid, undesirable, or not-yet-validated states impossible to represent at compile time.
keyFeatures: Compile-time typestate enforcement via proc-macro attributes
State-specific data that only exists in the states where it is valid
Typed rehydration from persisted rows back into legal machine states
Builder-first API with zero runtime overhead for state transitions
Async validator support for database-backed state reconstruction
Batch rehydration with per-item or shared machine context
Event-log projection helpers for append-only storage patterns
Nested machine composition for hierarchical workflows
publicApiSurface: #[state] — defines lifecycle phases as an enum of typed state markers
#[machine] — defines a struct carrying durable context across all states
#[transition] — annotates impl blocks that define legal state transitions
#[validators(Machine)] — annotates impl blocks for typed rehydration from persisted data
StateMarker — trait implemented by all generated state marker types
UnitState — trait for unit (payload-free) state markers
DataState — trait for data-bearing state markers
statum::Error::InvalidState — returned when no validator matches during rehydration
statum::Result<T> — convenience alias for Result<T, statum::Error>
projection::ProjectionReducer — trait for folding event streams into row snapshots
projection::reduce_one — reduces a single event stream into a projection row
projection::reduce_grouped — reduces interleaved event streams keyed by entity id
